data_in.description: Parsed MS/MS spectrum data (precursor m/z, ionization mode, fragment peak list with m/z and intensity)

data_out.description: Molecular fingerprint prediction response from CSI:FingerID web service (structured JSON or table format containing fingerprint bits and confidence scores)

domain_knowledge: CSI:FingerID is a web service component of SIRIUS that predicts molecular fingerprints from MS/MS spectra; it requires properly formatted precursor m/z, ionization mode, and fragment peak data.
The SIRIUS web services are restricted to academic research and education use only; non-academic users must obtain licenses from Bright Giant GmbH.
Fingerprint predictions from CSI:FingerID are returned as structured JSON responses that encode molecular structural features as bit vectors or scored predictions.

direct_checks: verify that github:sirius-ms__sirius repository is accessible and contains CSI:FingerID implementation code
verify that a working CSI:FingerID web service endpoint URL is documented or discoverable in the repository
file_exists: check that example spectrum input files or test data are present in the repository (e.g., .ms, .mgf, or JSON spectrum formats)
script_runs: verify that a parsing and submission script can be executed without runtime errors on sample spectrum data
output_matches_reference: verify that fingerprint prediction output from the endpoint matches expected JSON schema (contains predicted fingerprint scores or feature vectors)
expert_review: assess whether the fingerprint predictions returned by CSI:FingerID are chemically plausible and rank known correct structures appropriately
evaluate whether request-response latency and service availability meet documented SLA or expected performance characteristics
review whether authentication, rate-limiting, or access control on the endpoint is properly documented and respected

cmd: import json, sys; import urllib.request; spectrum_data = json.load(open('{inputs.spectrum_query}')); payload = json.dumps(spectrum_data).encode('utf-8'); req = urllib.request.Request('https://www.csi-fingerid.uni-jena.de/api/fingerid/query', data=payload, headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'}); response = urllib.request.urlopen(req); result = json.load(response); json.dump(result, sys.stdout, indent=2)

task_description: Submit a parsed spectrum query (with precursor m/z, ionization mode, and MS/MS fragment peaks) to the CSI:FingerID web service endpoint and retrieve the molecular fingerprint prediction response.

task_inputs.label: Parsed MS/MS spectrum with precursor and fragments

task_objective: Implement a client function that submits a spectrum query to the CSI:FingerID endpoint and parses the returned molecular fingerprint predictions.
title: Reconstruct the CSI:FingerID web-service dispatch for molecular fingerprint prediction
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Prepare spectrum metadata (precursor m/z, ionization mode, collision energy if available) and fragment peak list (m/z and intensity pairs). 2. Construct a valid CSI:FingerID web service request payload conforming to the SIRIUS API specification. 3. Submit the HTTP POST request to the CSI:FingerID endpoint via the SIRIUS web service gateway. 4. Parse the JSON response to extract the predicted molecular fingerprint representation and associated confidence or scoring metrics. 5. Save the fingerprint prediction output in a structured format (JSON or CSV) for downstream analysis.
